title: 2025 03 01 Whoa Lets Think About What Just Happening Dude You Go To Pea
html:
TL;DR

  • After 10 months of zero contact post-breakup, Sam saw Yahir in line at Peacock Bar and her group escalated immediately to physical confrontation.
  • Sam's male companion threw closed-fist punches at Yahir's face; Sam hammered the back of his head and then deployed an illegal taser into his back, holding the trigger ~10 seconds.
  • Bouncers piled on, restrained Yahir on a car, four people pinned him; he describes going calm and rational, waiting for cops.
  • Police questioned him, vitals were checked at the station, ~30–45 min process; Yahir was called a rapist during the incident — claim he treats as defamatory.
  • Yahir's framing: he called his friend before arriving, Sam saw the missed call and "went into a frenzy" allegedly threatening retaliation if he showed.

Notes

This is the first concrete written record in the vault of the Peacock Bar incident — referenced obliquely across many later Sam entries but here Yahir tries to reconstruct the actual sequence of events on the morning after. He toggles between two registers: a stream-of-consciousness first-person retelling ("you go to Peacock… my series is kind of mad") and then a cleaner second draft framing ("after a peaceful breakup and absolute zero contact for 10 months").

The note matters because it timestamps the night that becomes the recurring referent for the Sam arc — the moment the relationship turns from breakup-grief into something with legal/physical edges. Connect to the broader Sam thread and the Austin geography (Peacock is a Sixth Street bar Yahir kept ending up at). The taser detail and the "called a rapist" defamation claim are the load-bearing facts the later entries lean on.

Connections

  • sam — the central recurring entities node; this is the night that re-anchors the arc.
  • peacock bar — first detailed entry tying this venue to the sam confrontation.
  • austin tx — geographic locus.
  • yahir family — Yahir is the subject; the post-incident frame ("I'm rational afterwards") feeds his self-image.
